title: Difficulty-conditioned per-phase token budgets + orchestrator stop-losses: triage's risk/complexity classification scales downstream thinking budgets (~2K-class easy / 8K+-class hard, NEVER uniform); orchestrator enforces deterministic kill/retry on runaway phases + per-phase/lane token attribution (30x same-task variance; caps are quality-POSITIVE)
summary: Research findings #6+#7+#9 (knowledge-base/research/token-optimization-2026/README.md, applicability C2/D1/D2): (a) budget-conditioned prompting cuts 68-74% of tokens at <5% accuracy loss (TALE arXiv 2412.18547, SelfBudgeter 2505.11274) BUT uniform caps measurably harm hard tasks (~4-pt AIME drops, DART 2511.01170) - budgets must scale with difficulty; (b) identical agentic runs vary 1x-30x in token use and models cannot predict their own consumption (r<=0.39), so budget enforcement must be DETERMINISTIC orchestrator code (arXiv 2604.22750, 2605.09104); (c) accuracy PEAKS at intermediate token spend - beyond the optimum, more spend correlates with WORSE outcomes, so stop-losses improve quality, not just cost; (d) reflection value collapses after 1-2 passes - cap audit-loop re-entry at 1 unless explicit failure signals.
root_cause: No difficulty signal flows from triage to downstream phase execution: every cycle's phases run with the same unbounded-or-global limits regardless of task complexity, and no orchestrator-level stop-loss exists - a runaway phase (30x variance is empirical) burns quota until phase timeout, and audit loops can re-enter without an iteration cap.
fix: Config-driven, deterministic (Rule 5 - budgets are regular code, not prompts): (1) triage emits a difficulty/risk class per top_n task (it already assesses complexity; surface it in triage-decision.json). (2) policy.json gains a budget matrix: difficulty class x phase -> token budget class (easy ~2K-thinking-class for scout/triage, hard 8K+ for builder/tdd); zero Go literals. (3) Orchestrator threads the budget into phase launch (max-token contract where the driver supports it; prompt budget-line 'target <= N tokens' per TALE otherwise) and enforces a STOP-LOSS: phase exceeding its class budget by a configured factor -> deterministic kill -> failure-adapter retry at escalated effort/budget (composes with per-phase-effort-routing). (4) Audit-family re-entry capped at 1 iteration unless a NEW failure signal. (5) Surface per-phase/per-lane token attribution from existing usage artifacts into the cycle dossier (measurement baseline the research mandates).
acceptance: triage-decision.json carries a difficulty class per task; budget matrix lives in policy.json (no Go literals).
A phase exceeding its difficulty-conditioned budget is killed and retried deterministically (regression test with a fake runaway phase).
Uniform-cap regression guard: hard-class tasks get the HIGHER budget (test asserts easy!=hard budgets).
Audit-family re-entry capped at 1 without new failure signals.
Cycle dossier includes per-phase input/output token attribution.
connects_to: token-optimization research README findings #6/#7/#9, applicability C2/D1/D2
per-phase-effort-routing (the actuator this budget system drives)
quota_dynamic_budgeting (fleet-level budget spine; this is the PHASE-level analog)
notes: Injected 2026-07-05, research-derived batch 2/2. Weight 0.87: quality-positive by evidence (accuracy peaks at intermediate spend); the ONE hard caution is encoded as an acceptance criterion (never uniform caps). Evidence: TALE 68% cut <5% loss; SelfBudgeter 74%; DART uniform-cap harm; 2604.22750 30x variance + intermediate-spend peak; 2605.09104 stop-loss recommendation; reflection economics (zylos synthesis).
